title: The Birdcage Keeps Closing
description: Pages 1–5 focus on the birdcage keeps closing, keeping the Dressrosa conflict and character roles clear for display.
scene: This part follows Pica, Kyros, Unknown person #1, Unknown person #2, Unknown person #3, Unknown person #4, Unknown person #5 as even as executives fall, the Birdcage keeps shrinking and turns the whole country into a race against time.

dialogue.speaker: Pica
dialogue.text: My apologies, King Riku! Our desperate search was in vain...

dialogue.speaker: Kyros
dialogue.text: We were beaten to the punch by a man named Diamante...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Memories of that day come to mind...

dialogue.speaker: Kyros
dialogue.text: Lady scarlet, she... she was found in the city, shot!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: Ah, so that was your sister!?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: She was stocking up on food in the city market!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: If she just ignored her stomach for a little longer, she'd still be alive today! The fool. Haha!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #8
dialogue.text: We soldiers will remain in the Palace! We will protect you!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #12
dialogue.text: I am in your debt, Robi- Land.

dialogue.speaker: Kyros
dialogue.text: HAA... HAA... FATHER! You must get those wounds treated!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #18
dialogue.text: Rebec- CA's also unharmed! Nico Robin protected her!

dialogue.speaker: Colosseum Fighters
dialogue.text: The family's top executive!? The hero of the colosseum!? Really!?

dialogue.speaker: Diamante
dialogue.text: What are you talking about!? kyros is the original, undefeated, hero of the colosseum!

dialogue.speaker: Doflamingo Family
dialogue.text: The Donquijote Family that we thought so unbeat- able...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #21
dialogue.text: Is crumbling, one member at a time!

summary: The Birdcage Keeps Closing advances the Dressrosa story through Pica, Kyros, Unknown person #1, Unknown person #2, Unknown person #3, with unclear voices kept readable rather than forced into unsafe speaker assignments.
